topic: Sharp minimax ATE estimation with unrestricted high-dimensional discrete confounders: for iid (X,A,Y) with X in [d], binary A and Y, overlap epsilon <= P(A=1|X=k) <= 1-epsilon, and unrestricted cell probabilities and conditional response means, prove that the exact minimax MSE is asymptotically equivalent up to epsilon-dependent constants to 1/n + [d/(n log n)]^2 uniformly for d <= c n log n; construct a computable hybrid estimator using plug-in estimation on well-populated cells and polynomial/factorial-moment estimation on sparsely populated cells, pair it with the published moment-matching lower bound, and establish parametric MSE through d of order sqrt(n) log n and consistency through d=o(n log n), thereby closing the log-squared gap left by Zeng-Balakrishnan-Han-Kennedy (arXiv:2405.00118). Delegate the exact heavy/light split, polynomial basis and degree constants, Poissonization details, and variance normalization to derivation, but require a proved light-cell approximation-and-variance lemma and a verified two-category confounding witness.

open_problems.open_problem: Does the unrestricted d-cell binary-treatment, binary-outcome overlap model have minimax ATE MSE of order 1/n + [d/(n log n)]^2 uniformly for d up to a constant times n log n, and can a computable heavy-cell plug-in/light-cell polynomial estimator attain it?

source_refs.claim: The paper proves MSE at most d^2/n^2+1/n for standard estimators and a lower bound d^2/(n^2 log^2 n)+1/n, then explicitly leaves polynomial ATE estimators and closure of the gap to future work.
source_refs.author: Zhenghao Zeng, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, Yanjun Han, Edward H. Kennedy

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published lower bound uses moment matching to obtain an effective n log n sample-size enlargement, while every analyzed causal estimator first estimates cellwise regressions or propensities and pays the ordinary d/n bias scale. Polynomial estimators close analogous gaps for additive distribution functionals, but the ATE is a coupled rational functional of the joint (X,A,Y) cell probabilities: in sparse cells one must approximate inverse treatment-arm masses while preserving overlap, estimate the resulting monomials by factorial moments, and control aggregate variance under an unrestricted and highly nonuniform covariate law. No published result supplies the required light-cell approximation-and-variance argument.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A matching minimax upper-and-lower MSE theorem, an explicit computable heavy/light estimator, and a uniform lemma bounding light-cell polynomial approximation bias and factorial-moment variance.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the exact observational model, explains why sparsity and smoothness do not apply to categorical confounders, and ends with the upper/lower logarithmic mismatch.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: many categorical confounders defeat smooth or sparse nuisance models -> standard causal estimators incur d^2/n^2 MSE -> moment matching lowers the information bound by log^2 n -> can a polynomial rare-cell estimator attain that lower bound?

method_exemplars.technique: Use a soft sparse/dense regime split, polynomial or linear-program approximation in sparse cells, and a bias-corrected plug-in rule in dense cells, with a direct global MSE proof.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It treats discrete rational-function estimation with the hybrid sparse/dense architecture needed for the ATE cell functional.

method_exemplars.technique: Approximate a nonsmooth cell functional by a best polynomial, estimate its monomials unbiasedly with Poisson factorial moments, and combine it with a bias-corrected plug-in estimator on the smooth region.
method_exemplars.why_closest: This is the canonical proof that an n-sample polynomial estimator can mimic a plug-in estimator with n log n samples, the amplification required by the published lower bound.

method_exemplars.technique: Pair best uniform polynomial approximation with unbiased factorial-moment estimation and use approximation/moment duality to match the minimax lower bound.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its entropy risk exhibits the same n-to-n log n enlargement and provides a clean template for balancing polynomial bias against coefficient-driven variance.

open_problems.open_problem: Can one construct honest confidence intervals for the unrestricted discrete-confounder ATE whose length matches n^(-1/2)+d/(n log n), including the non-root-n regime where sparse-cell polynomial error dominates?

source_refs.claim: The paper's ATE central limit theorem is for fixed d, warns that growing d breaks the fixed-dimensional Donsker argument, and notes that its high-dimensional confidence intervals may miss nominal coverage.
source_refs.author: Zhenghao Zeng, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, Yanjun Han, Edward H. Kennedy

source_refs.claim: The paper proves a slower-than-root-n central limit theorem for undersmoothed double-cross-fit doubly robust estimation under smooth regression structure, not an unrestricted growing discrete alphabet.
source_refs.author: Alec McClean, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, Edward H. Kennedy, Larry Wasserman

open_problems.why_unsolved: An MSE-optimal candidate necessarily includes a data-dependent sparse-cell polynomial component whose approximation bias can be the same order as its standard deviation and whose variance is driven by many heterogeneous low-count factorial moments. Classical influence-function standard errors omit this component, while existing non-root-n causal central limit theorems rely on smooth regression operators and undersmoothing. Uniformity across the transition from root-n to rare-cell-dominated behavior therefore requires a new bias normalization and triangular-array variance argument.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniform self-normalized limit theorem or an honest confidence-set construction with coverage at least 1-alpha and expected length of order n^(-1/2)+d/(n log n).

writing_exemplar.why: Its model, point-risk phase transition, fixed-d central limit theorem, and coverage warning give a direct path from estimation to the missing uncertainty quantification.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: high-dimensional categorical adjustment creates nonparametric ATE rates -> point-estimation bounds expose a sparse-cell regime -> fixed-d influence-function inference no longer applies -> how can uncertainty be quantified at the sharp rare-cell rate?

method_exemplars.technique: Decompose an independently fitted doubly robust statistic, undersmooth its bias, estimate its nonstandard variance, and prove a slower-than-root-n central limit theorem.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is a direct causal example of valid Gaussian inference below the root-n rate, although smooth-bias control must be replaced by polynomial approximation control.

method_exemplars.technique: Use empirical higher-order-influence-function U-statistics, a Hoeffding decomposition, projection-kernel variance calculations, and studentization for an ATE-type nonlinear functional.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A rare-cell factorial-moment correction is likewise a higher-order statistic, so its variance and studentization proof is the closest causal analogue.

open_problems.open_problem: When overlap deteriorates as epsilon_n approaches zero, what is the minimax ATE MSE as a joint function of n, d, and epsilon_n in the unrestricted discrete-confounder model, and where are the consistency and root-n phase boundaries?

source_refs.claim: The discrete-confounder minimax results impose a fixed overlap constant and absorb its effect into constants, so they do not determine a joint d-versus-n-versus-epsilon_n phase diagram.
source_refs.author: Zhenghao Zeng, Sivaraman Balakrishnan, Yanjun Han, Edward H. Kennedy

source_refs.claim: The paper shows that sufficiently weak overlap prevents regular root-n ATE estimation and derives thresholded doubly robust asymptotic normality under tail and nuisance-rate restrictions.
source_refs.author: Jacob Dorn

source_refs.claim: The paper characterizes bounded-outcome ATE partial identification under actual non-overlap, providing the endpoint a deteriorating-overlap theory must approach.
source_refs.author: Herbert P. Susmann, Alec McClean, Ivan Diaz

open_problems.why_unsolved: Fixed-overlap discrete minimax theory and weak-overlap semiparametric theory vary different axes. In a growing alphabet, a treatment-arm cell can be rare because its covariate mass is small, its propensity is small, or both; factorial-moment variances depend on products of these probabilities, while inverse-propensity amplification interacts with the n log n effective-sample phenomenon. Weak-overlap results encode rarity through propensity tails in a different nuisance model, whereas the discrete results hide epsilon in constants, so neither determines the joint phase diagram or its limiting connection to partial identification.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A matching minimax risk formula R(n,d,epsilon_n), with an attaining estimator and lower bound that locate the root-n, slow-rate consistent, inconsistent, and zero-overlap partial-identification endpoint regimes.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the clearest narrative from overlap deterioration to loss of regular root-n ATE inference while retaining the original full-population target.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: inverse-propensity estimation is stable under strong overlap -> weak overlap destroys regular root-n behavior -> thresholding recovers inference under tail control -> how does that boundary change when the confounder alphabet also grows?

method_exemplars.technique: Retain epsilon_n explicitly in the multinomial or Poissonized cell construction, moment-matching priors, and cellwise risk calculation instead of absorbing it into constants.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its lower-bound experiment is the required large-alphabet starting point for a joint phase diagram.

method_exemplars.technique: Threshold inverse-propensity contributions, quantify truncation bias against overlap tails, and studentize the retained score.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Thresholding is the treatment-arm analogue of a heavy/light cell split and exposes how deteriorating overlap changes bias and variance.

method_exemplars.technique: Decompose the ATE into an identified overlap-region contribution and bounded worst-case contributions outside overlap.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It characterizes the zero-overlap endpoint to which the joint minimax frontier should connect.

literature_map:
Zeng, Balakrishnan, Han and Kennedy (2024, arXiv:2405.00118) are the direct anchor: analyzed upper MSE d^2/n^2+1/n versus lower MSE d^2/(n^2 log^2 n)+1/n.
Their current discussion explicitly asks for polynomial-approximation ATE estimators realizing effective sample-size enlargement and notes that the bounds do not match.
Their fixed-d central limit theorem and high-dimensional coverage warning leave inference in the growing-alphabet regime unresolved.
Jiao, Venkat, Han and Weissman (2015, IEEE TIT, arXiv:1406.6956) give the canonical nonsmooth-polynomial/smooth-plug-in estimator for large-alphabet functionals.
Wu and Yang (2016, IEEE TIT, arXiv:1407.0381) match entropy bounds through best-polynomial-approximation/moment duality and obtain n log n amplification.
Wu and Yang (2019, Annals of Statistics, arXiv:1504.01227) use Chebyshev coefficients and moment matching to estimate unseen support with a computable linear statistic.
Han, Jiao and Weissman (2020, IEEE JSAIT, arXiv:1605.09124) treat discrete rational divergences with sparse/dense splitting and polynomial correction.
Orlitsky, Suresh and Wu (2016, arXiv:1511.07428) show smoothed Good-Toulmin estimators can extrapolate unseen mass to a logarithmically larger sample.
Jiao and Han (2017, arXiv:1709.06183) show that uncontrolled iterated bootstrap or jackknife bias correction can inflate error in binomial functional estimation.
Robins, Li, Tchetgen Tchetgen and van der Vaart (2008, arXiv:0805.3040) develop higher-order U-statistic corrections for minimax causal and missing-data functionals.
Liu, Mukherjee, Newey and Robins (2017, arXiv:1705.07577) provide an empirical higher-order-influence-function and inference template.
McClean, Balakrishnan, Kennedy and Wasserman (2024, arXiv:2403.15175) prove slower-than-root-n causal central limit theory under smooth regression structure.
Dorn (2025, arXiv:2504.13273) develops thresholded doubly robust inference under weak overlap, exposing a second axis absent from fixed-epsilon discrete minimax theory.
Susmann, McClean and Diaz (2025, arXiv:2509.20206) characterize the bounded-outcome partial-identification endpoint when overlap actually fails.
The live tensions are therefore upper-versus-lower point risk, point estimation versus honest inference, and alphabet growth versus deteriorating overlap.
prior_proposal_map:
The exact qid stat_discrete_ate_minimax_loggap currently has only state and pipeline initialization; no proposal, rejected angle, or reviewer JSON was available to mine.
The closest ATE-minimax catalogue entry was stat_ate_overlap_decay/v1, including archived rejected angles and six angle-review JSONs.
Its failed-bank README identifies a class-uniform envelope comparison and shell-matching converse as the remaining blocker outside the root-n regime.
Archived scope flags preserve adaptation, boundary inference, and higher-order-influence-function optimality as follow-up questions.
Reviewer strengthening_paths repeatedly requested an explicit least-favorable two-law exhibit with likelihood-distance and ATE-separation arithmetic.
The adjacent stat_gformula_composition_minimax run contributed rank-safe attaining-estimator and approximate-state-sufficiency suggestions, but these concern longitudinal composition rather than the discrete-cell log gap.
No prior proposal mentioning Zeng-Balakrishnan-Han-Kennedy, arXiv:2405.00118, factorial moments, or the d/(n log n) frontier was found.
Accordingly, none of the Track-A candidates was cross-linked or retained in the topic-specific G1-G5 ledger.
